name: Pumpkin Light Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Granulated artificial sweetener OR 2 tablespoons Liquid Sugar Twin
1 1/4 cups Canned pumpkin (or cooked) or 2 tablespoons Sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pie crust by melting butter in a 9-inch pie plate at high heat for 30 to 40 seconds. Add Graham wafer crumbs and combine with a fork.
2. Microwave the mixture at high for 3 to 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching. Let cool before filling.
3. Dissolve gelatin in cold water and set aside.
4. In a separate bowl, beat egg yolks, milk (or skim milk), canned pumpkin or sugar, salt, and spices until well combined.
5. Microwave the mixture at medium-low for 30-second intervals, stirring after each interval, until thick and smooth. Add gelatin and sweetener, mixing until completely dissolved.
6. Refrigerate until set to the consistency of beaten egg whites.
7. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add sugar and continue beating until stiff and shiny.
8. Gently fold the whipped egg white mixture into the pumpkin mixture, ensuring thorough incorporation.
9. Pour the batter into the prepared pie shell and chill overnight.
1/8 pie - 168 calories, 16 grams carbohydrate, 5 grams protein, 8 grams fat; 1 starch, 1 protein, 1 fat & oils choice
Source: More Choice, the Canadian Diabetic Micro. Cookbook 1991 Catha McMaster & Charlotte Empringham
